user_prompt: linker errors indicating a ton of stuff didn't get compiled
collect2.exe: error: ld returned 1 exit status
agent_reply: 1. `AudioManager` could include `bluedroid_sbc.h` because `platformio.ini` had global `-I lib/bluedroid...` paths, but PlatformIO was not linking the `lib/bluedroid` library.
2. Once forced into the build, the local Bluedroid decoder sources conflicted with decoder symbols already present in ESP32’s built-in `libbt.a`.
- In [platformio.ini](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini), added the local library as an explicit dependency:
- Kept the global bluedroid include paths so `AudioManager` can compile.
- In [lib/bluedroid/library.json](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/bluedroid/library.json), made the library self-contained with include flags.
- Removed local decoder `.c` files from `srcFilter`; the build now uses ESP32’s built-in Bluedroid decoder objects and only compiles the wrapper plus encoder sources.

user_prompt: C:/Users/frank/.platformio/packages/toolchain-xtensa-esp-elf/bin/../lib/gcc/xtensa-esp-elf/13.2.0/../../../../xtensa-esp-elf/bin/ld.exe: .pio/build/theflybringuptest/firmware.elf section `.iram0.text' will not fit in region `iram0_0_seg'
C:/Users/frank/.platformio/packages/toolchain-xtensa-esp-elf/bin/../lib/gcc/xtensa-esp-elf/13.2.0/../../../../xtensa-esp-elf/bin/ld.exe: IRAM0 segment data does not fit.
collect2.exe: error: ld returned 1 exit status
ouch, we haven't even implemented half of the project yet
agent_reply: The mSBC undefined symbol is gone by changing [bluedroid_sbc.c](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/bluedroid/bluedroid_sbc.c) to use `OI_CODEC_SBC_DecoderReset(...)` and then set `mSBCEnabled = TRUE`, instead of calling the missing `OI_CODEC_mSBC_DecoderReset`.

The IRAM overflow was the ESP32 classic BT plus PSRAM combo. I disabled `BOARD_HAS_PSRAM` only for `[env:theflybringuptest]` in [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini), which frees enough IRAM for this bringup test without changing the base env.
Also finished the previous cleanup: `print_bda` is gone from the BT tests; they use `log_bda`.
Done. I fixed the `platformio.ini` path and the build fallout around the HCI SCO config.
- Added the HCI SCO Bluetooth sdkconfig overrides in `platformio.ini`.
- Added a pre-build script to generate the managed component cert `.S` files PlatformIO expected.
- Removed/renamed project headers that were shadowing libsodium internals:
- Fixed M5GFX’s missing ESP LCD include path.
- Fixed `lib/bluedroid` so the mSBC decoder sources and includes actually build/link.
- Removed the stale `--wrap=log_printf` linker flag since ESP Insights is now disabled.
There are still a few third-party warnings, but no build errors.

user_prompt: if our copy inside `lib\bluedroid\encoder` and `lib\bluedroid\decoder`, then block our own copy and use the included one from esp-idf
agent_reply: Done. I blocked our local `lib/bluedroid/encoder` and `lib/bluedroid/decoder` sources from compiling and switched the wrapper to use ESP-IDF’s included Bluedroid SBC symbols instead.
- `platformio.ini` now exposes the ESP-IDF BT/SBC include paths
- Removed the earlier local symbol-prefix workaround

I also fixed the wrapper for the ESP-IDF encoder shape, including copying PCM into `as16PcmBuffer` when `SBC_NO_PCM_CPY_OPTION` is false. That is likely the actual crash fix for the `EncPacking` StoreProhibited path.
- The map file now shows SBC encoder/decoder symbols coming from ESP-IDF `libbt.a`, while our library only contributes `bluedroid_sbc.c` as the adapter.

I also fixed the wrapper for the ESP-IDF encoder shape, including copying PCM into `as16PcmBuffer` when `SBC_NO_PCM_CPY_OPTION` is false. That is likely the actual crash fix for the `EncPacking` StoreProhibited path.
- The map file now shows SBC encoder/decoder symbols coming from ESP-IDF `libbt.a`, while our library only contributes `bluedroid_sbc.c` as the adapter.

user_prompt: review platformio.ini and `lib\ESP-FTP-Server-Lib\library.json` and `lib\ESP-FTP-Server-Lib\platformio.ini` and make sure this builds right now with no code changes
agent_reply: 1. **The default project build does not succeed right now.**

I ran `pio run -e thefly`, which matches the default environment in [platformio.ini](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:2). The build gets through compilation and fails at link time with an IRAM overflow:
.iram0.text will not fit in region iram0_0_seg
IRAM0 segment data does not fit
So the current PlatformIO/library setup is not a dependency-resolution blocker, but the project still does not produce a firmware image right now.
2. **The FTP library is being picked up from the local `lib/` copy as intended.**

In the tested build, PlatformIO compiled objects from `lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib`, including the FTP server sources. The root [platformio.ini](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:121) no longer lists the registry dependency for `kastenklicker/ESP-FTP-Server`, so the vendored library is the one in play for this build.
3. **The vendored library metadata disagrees with itself.**
That mismatch did **not** stop this PlatformIO build from finding and compiling the library, but it is worth cleaning up later because it makes the library identity/version ambiguous.
4. **The library’s own `platformio.ini` is not the parent build configuration.**

[lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/platformio.ini](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/platformio.ini:1) defines standalone library test/example environments. The parent firmware build uses the root [platformio.ini](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:1), so the vendored library’s `platformio.ini` is not what controls `pio run -e thefly`.
5. **There is a non-fatal FTP library compatibility warning.**

The build warns at [ESP-FTP-Server-Lib.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib.cpp:40) that `NetworkServer::available()` is deprecated and has been renamed to `accept()`. That is not today’s failure, but it is a sign the vendored library is rubbing against the current networking API.
- I reviewed and built without making code changes.
- I treated `thefly` as the build that matters because it is the root config’s `default_envs`.
- The immediate failure is memory placement at link time, not a malformed `library.json` or a bad `lib_deps` entry.

The PlatformIO setup is currently resolving the local `lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib` and compiling it. The build still fails now because `thefly` exceeds IRAM by 2920 bytes. The metadata mismatch between `library.json` and `library.properties` is a cleanup item, but it is not the build-stopper I observed.

user_prompt: was there a reason why we used `KastenKlicker`'s fork? is it because it's made to work on platformio?
agent_reply: From what is in the repo:
- The local `library.json` points at **KastenKlicker’s** repo and has PlatformIO metadata: [library.json](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/library.json:1).
- The Arduino-style metadata still points at **Peter Buchegger’s** original repo: [library.properties](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/library.properties:1).
- Before vendoring it locally, your root `platformio.ini` had the dependency form:
That is a PlatformIO Registry package name, so KastenKlicker’s fork was the directly convenient PlatformIO dependency.
1. **Peterus appears to be the original library lineage.**
2. **KastenKlicker’s fork/package is the PlatformIO-facing version you pulled in.**
3. We did not choose it for an SdFat feature or passive FTP feature. We chose it because it was the library/package already being resolved by PlatformIO in this project.
It is also plausible KastenKlicker carried newer ESP32 compatibility or registry maintenance beyond the original Arduino package, but I have not compared the upstream histories yet.
